caption: This educational presentation on "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" by Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay, explains the Linde-Hampson System using T-s diagrams. The slides feature a plot of temperature (T) versus entropy (s), depicting various thermodynamic processes with labeled points and paths. The first frame describes the isothermal compression from $1 \rightarrow 2$ and isenthalpic expansion from $3 \rightarrow 4$, noting that gas and liquid states are given by "g" and "f". The second frame addresses real-world imperfections where heat exchange is not perfect, changing processes to $2 \rightarrow 3'$ and $g \rightarrow 1'$. The third frame highlights that gas is compressed from state $1'$ to state $2$, with expansion occurring from $3' \rightarrow 4'$. The final frame concludes that in an ideal system, enthalpy changes on isobaric lines are equal, providing the formula for effectiveness as $\varepsilon = \frac{h_1 - h_2}{h_1 - h_g}$. The "NPTEL" logo appears in the corner of each plot.
